title: New Air Force One Lacks Defensive Countermeasures of Previous Model, Officials Say
summary: Experts said the lack of such capabilities poses a potential risk when the president travels overseas. The White House defended the aircraft’s safety.

title: How Trump Is Trying to Tip the Scales for the Midterms, and Why Grocery Stores Are Scrambling
summary: Plus, the Friday news quiz.

title: Iran’s Supreme Leader Remains Absent, a Void at the Top of the Regime
summary: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei did not appear at his father’s funeral this week, fueling speculation about his physical condition and leaving a power vacuum in a divided country.

title: The U.S. and Iran Are Fighting Again. Is the Cease-Fire Over?
summary: Eric Schmitt, a New York Times National Security correspondent, analyzes recent skirmishes between the U.S. and Iran and whether they threaten ongoing negotiations for a peace deal.

title: Bombing Iran Didn’t Work for Trump. Neither Did a Tentative Cease-Fire. Is There a Plan C?
summary: The administration appears to be reverting to an all-stick, no-carrot approach. But it has yet to answer why it believes economic warfare and bombing will yield a different result this time.

title: Mediators Trying to Pull U.S. and Iran Back From Brink, Officials Say
summary: Days of strikes by the two countries have given way to an uneasy pause as Qatari mediators seek to salvage the tattered U.S.-Iran truce.

title: Mexican Man Fatally Shot by ICE Wasn’t the Intended Target
summary: Officials say agents believed a passenger resembled one suspect, but the encounter quickly escalated into a fatal shooting.

title: What We Know About the ICE Shooting of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo
summary: Mr. Araujo was a father, a husband and a business owner who had moved to the United States 35 years ago from Mexico.

title: Claims of Poor Conditions in ICE Facility Are Bolstered by Health Inspectors
summary: Detainees at the Delaney Hall center in Newark have complained for months about dirty, unhealthy living conditions. Documents echo their claims.

title: Why Some Women in Maine Are Mourning the End of Graham Platner’s Campaign
summary: They believe the accuser, but they also grieve the demise of a campaign that promised that politics could be different — and they blame those who failed to find a less flawed candidate.

title: Trump Administration Fires Members of Independent Election Group
summary: The firings and a resignation render the Election Assistance Commission useless. The moves come as President Trump seeks to impose control over how ballots will be counted in the midterms.

title: Nine Years Later, They’re Still Restoring a House Bought at Auction
summary: First-time buyers from the Bay Area won an abandoned house at a Kingston, N.Y. tax auction. Eight years later, they’re still restoring it.

title: Bayeux Tapestry Arrives in U.K. After Maximum-Security Operation
summary: The 11th-century tapestry will go on display at the British Museum in September. Tickets for the exhibition have already been selling rapidly.

title: How American Socialism Changed, and Stormed the Democratic Party
summary: The movement was better at critiquing the system than reshaping it. But it has never had this much mainstream political power.

title: At Least 12 People Die in Southern Spain Wildfire
summary: More than 20 people were still missing after the blaze in Andalusia, one of the deadliest on record in the country. Many of the victims were foreign tourists, the Spanish authorities said.

title: Fighting Fires With Figures, These Experts Are Trying to Stop Europe From Burning
summary: A team of engineers, foresters and scientists is helping the continent prepare for wildfires from a giant science park in Italy. Their arsenal includes satellites, weather models and expert analysis.

title: After Venezuela Earthquakes, a Youth Baseball League Counts the Dead
summary: Children who played for Venezuela’s youth baseball league, a symbol of national pride, have been left injured, orphaned or dead.

title: How Olivia Wilde Builds Comic Tension in ‘The Invite’
summary: The director and actress discusses a tense scene from her dinner-party comedy, in which she stars with Seth Rogen, Penélope Cruz and Edward Norton.

title: ‘The Invite’ Review: Who’s Afraid of a Last-Minute Dinner Party?
summary: In this witty comedy, Seth Rogen and Olivia Wilde star as a millennial couple whose tense marriage gets a wild jolt from their neighbors.

title: Jerrold Nadler: Penn Station Won’t Fund Itself
summary: Penn Station needs massive fixes, but the Trump administration is going about it all wrong.

title: The Very Good and Very Bad News on Climate
summary: Advancements in renewable energy are paving the way for a new climate politics. The environmentalist Bill McKibben articulates some of the possibilities in this new era of energy abundance.

title: The Housing Crisis
summary: Happy Friday. I want to tell you about a rare instance of bipartisan cooperation in Congress.

title: Ronny Chieng Tackles Trump's Two Air Force Ones
summary: “Is that why there’s no gas in the world anymore? Because the president flies with an extra emotional support plane?” Chieng said on “The Daily Show.”

title: Moscow Still Has Art and Culture. Just Don’t Say ‘Ukraine.’
summary: An eerie limbo prevails at small art shows, independent theaters and private political clubs, where the war is the elephant in the room.
